chunk_summary: This section provides clinical vignettes illustrating decision-making in outpatient obesity management, focusing on tailored interventions and follow-up strategies for diverse pediatric cases.
learning_objectives: Understand sequenced outpatient pathways for managing pediatric obesity across varied scenarios.
Identify red flags and prioritize metabolic screening in obesity-related complications.
Apply responsive feeding, structured lifestyle interventions, and co-management strategies in complex cases.
key_facts: Preschool vignette emphasizes stopping bedtime calorie liquids and structured meals/snacks.
Adolescent hostel vignette targets sugar-sweetened beverage elimination and shorter follow-up intervals.
Antipsychotic-associated weight gain requires co-management with psychiatry and metabolic screening for dysglycemia and lipid abnormalities.
Suspected idiopathic intracranial hypertension (IIH) mandates urgent neuroimaging and ophthalmology referral.
LOC eating in adolescents may require screening for eating disorders per NICE guideline NG69.
reference_quote: ‘The Indian Academy of Pediatrics guidelines emphasize structured lifestyle interventions and early screening for metabolic risks in childhood obesity.’ (Reference 1)
